user_prompt: hmmm I got this build error now

C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: .pio/build/thefly_sec0/lib028/libNetworkClientSecure.a(NetworkClientSecure.cpp.o): in function `NetworkClientSecure::setCACertBundle(unsigned char const*, unsigned int)':
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
does this have something to do with `managed_components`?
is `tools\embed_managed_component_certs.py` not having an effect anymore?
agent_reply: 1. `libmbedtls.a` was in the wrong shape in the uploaded package.
- `libmbedtls.a` = ESP-IDF mbedTLS component wrapper, with `esp_crt_bundle.c.o` and `x509_crt_bundle.S.o`

2. The root-level `sdkconfig` in `framework-arduinoespressif32-libs` was triggering pioarduino’s “custom sdkconfig mismatch” logic. Since your current `platformio.ini` no longer has `custom_sdkconfig`, pioarduino deleted and reinstalled the libs package every build. That is exactly the “my settings got ignored” behavior.
I fixed the local package zips here:
- removed only the root `sdkconfig` from the libs zip
I also patched the installed `.platformio` package locally and verified:
So upload these rebuilt zips as a new release, preferably `r3`, and point `platformio.ini` at `r3`. Reusing `r2` may keep hitting PlatformIO/GitHub cache weirdness.

user_prompt: with this, everyhting seems to succeed, but I can't flash it, the `bootloader.bin` file is missing.
when I invoke a build through the IDE itself, I get to `Building .pio\build\thefly_sec0\bootloader.bin` in the terminal, and a notepad window pops up with `esptool.py`
I need to fix this, I think it's a call to build the `bootloader.bin` but it's calling it as if it was on unix when I am on windows
agent_reply: Yep, your read was right: it was calling `esptool.py` like an executable on Windows.
I fixed the custom framework package script so bootloader generation now uses:
when `$OBJCOPY` points to `esptool.py`, and I also changed the `elf2image` args from the old hyphen form to the esptool-supported underscore form:
I patched both local framework package trees and rebuilt:
I also patched the installed PlatformIO package locally and verified:
now succeeds, and this file exists:

Important: `platformio.ini` still points at `r3`. The installed local patch will work now, but a package reinstall from GitHub will bring the old `r3` framework back. Publish the rebuilt `framework-arduinoespressif32.zip` as `r4`, keep the already-fixed libs zip with it, then update both URLs in `platformio.ini` to `r4`.
